{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2008 Cannes Film Festival | image = 2008 Cannes Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 61st Cannes Film Festival featuring a photo of model ''Anouk Marguerite'' and photographed by ''Pierre Collier''.{{cite news|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2008/posters.html|title=Posters 2008|work=festival-cannes.fr|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131204193423/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2008/posters.html|archive-date=4 December 2013|df=dmy}} | host = [[Édouard Baer]] | number = 22 (Main Competition) | opening = ''[[Blindness (2008 film)|Blindness]]'' | closing = ''[[What Just Happened (2008 film)|What Just Happened]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | awards = ''[[Palme d'Or]]'':
''[[The Class (2008 film)|The Class]]'' | date = {{Start date|2008|05|14|df=y}} – {{End date|2008|05|25|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2007 Cannes Film Festival|2007]] | next = [[2009 Cannes Film Festival|2009]] }} The '''61st Annual [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 14 to 25 May 2008. American actor and filmmaker [[Sean Penn]] served as jury president for the main competition. French filmmaker [[Laurent Cantet]] won the [[Palme d'Or]], the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[The Class (2008 film)|The Class]]''.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2008.htm|title=61ème Festival International du Film - Cannes|work=cinema-francais.fr|language=fr|access-date=28 June 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.cannes-fest.com/cine/index.php?a=08|title=Cannes 2008 / Palmarés|work=cannes-fest.com|language=fr|access-date=28 June 2017}} The festival opened with ''[[Blindness (2008 film)|Blindness]]'' by [[Fernando Meirelles]]{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/starsandstories/3673209/Cannes-Film-Festival-2008-opener-the-end-of-civilisation.html|title=Cannes Film Festival 2008 opener: the end of civilisation|work=[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]]|date=9 May 2008|access-date=26 May 2017}} and closed with ''[[What Just Happened (2008 film)|What Just Happened]]'' by [[Barry Levinson]].{{cite web|url=http://voices.yahoo.com/2008-cannes-film-festival-lineup-1463981.html|title=2008 Cannes Film Festival Lineup|work=voices.yahoo.com|date=14 May 2008|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140728232348/http://voices.yahoo.com/2008-cannes-film-festival-lineup-1463981.html|archive-date=28 July 2014|df=dmy}} [[Édouard Baer]] was the master of ceremonies.{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/fr/article/56293.html|title=Edouard Baer, emcee of the 62nd Cannes Film Festival|work=festival-cannes.com|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224114058/http://www.festival-cannes.com/fr/article/56293.html|archive-date=24 December 2013|df=dmy}} The British press reported the list of films in competition this year was notable for its absence of British films for the second successive year.{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/film-and-tv/news/cannes-film-festival-snubs-british-movies-for-the-second-year-running-814741.html?r=RSS|title=Cannes Film Festival snubs British movies for the second year running|work=[[The Independent]]|date=23 April 2008 |access-date=26 May 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://entertainment.timesonline.co.uk/tol/arts_and_entertainment/film/article3803525.ece|title=Indiana Jones steals the show as Cannes ignores British films|work=[[The Times Literary Supplement]]|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110517101206/http://entertainment.timesonline.co.uk/tol/arts_and_entertainment/film/article3803525.ece|archive-date=17 May
